feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Vienna 2024 - Call For Speakers is now OPEN!!
entry.content:
After a long hiatus, the SAP Inside Track Vienna is BACK!!

The SAP Inside Track Vienna is a fantastic chance to share insights, exchange experiences, and connect with fellow SAP enthusiasts. We invite everyone to join our Call For Speakers and showcase their expertise. Whether you're an SAP guru, developer, consultant, architect or user, we’d love to hear from you!
Please see here for further details and for our guidelines regarding the submission process.

Interested in sponsoring the event? Have a look at our sponsoring options here.

Let's us make this a great and memorable event!

See you soon!
- the SITVIE team

entry.title: In a World deluged by Data, Clarity is Power!
entry.content:
Unlocking the Future of Data: Join Us Live to Explore Data as a Product and Democratization at SAP on September 5, 2024

In today’s fast-paced digital world, data is no longer just a byproduct of business operations; it has become one of the most valuable assets an organization can possess. But how do we harness this potential and turn data into a product that everyone in the organization can use to drive decision-making and innovation? SAP is leading the charge in this transformation, and we invite you to join us for an exclusive live session where we'll delve into this critical evolution.

Why You Can’t Miss This Session

The concept of "data as a product" is revolutionizing how businesses think about and utilize their data. It’s about more than just storing and managing data; it’s about treating data as an asset that can be packaged, shared, and consumed across the organization, empowering every employee to become a data-driven decision-maker.

During this live session, our expert Pinar Doelen, Chief Analytics Officer, will take you on a journey through SAP’s approach to data democratization, where we break down barriers and put powerful data analysis tools directly into the hands of citizen analysts—those everyday users who may not have formal training in data science but have the curiosity and business insight to drive impactful analysis.

 What You’ll Learn

  1. Data as a Product: Understand the fundamental shift towards treating data as a product. We’ll explore the data-product operating model that ensures data is accurate, reliable, and readily available to those who need it, when they need it.

  2. Data Democratization at SAP: Discover how SAP is fostering a culture of data democratization, enabling all employees—not just data specialists—to access, analyze, and act on data insights. This is about breaking down silos and making data accessible to everyone in the organization.

  3. Empowering Citizen Analysts: Learn how SAP’s IT organization is paving the way for citizen analysts to navigate through complex data sets with ease. With SAP Datasphere at the core, these tools are designed to scale and support widespread adoption, making it easier than ever for non-technical users to perform sophisticated analysis.

  4. SAP Datasphere: Dive into the capabilities of SAP Datasphere, the engine powering this revolution. From data integration to advanced analytics, SAP Datasphere provides the robust infrastructure needed to turn data into actionable insights at scale.

Who Should Attend

This session is perfect for IT leaders, data professionals, business analysts, and anyone interested in understanding how to leverage data more effectively within their organization. Whether you’re already on the journey towards data democratization or just beginning to explore the possibilities, this live session will provide valuable insights and practical strategies you can apply in your own business.

Don’t Miss Out—Join Us Live on September 5, 2024

This is more than just a webinar; it’s a deep dive into the future of data at SAP. By attending, you’ll gain firsthand knowledge of how to empower your team with the tools and mindset needed to harness the full potential of your data. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to engage with our expert, Pinar Doelen, Chief Digital Officer | Chief Information Officer | Chief Data and Analytics Officer at SAP, ask questions, and learn from real-world examples of how SAP is leading the way in data democratization.

Mark your calendar and join us live to explore how you can turn data into a product that drives innovation, agility, and competitive advantage. We look forward to seeing you there!

Join the Live Session on YouTube

Empower your organization. Democratize your data. Transform your future.

Explore entire series: https://url.sap/bnuoax 

Add to calendar: https://url.sap/66s5xu 

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: 如何在BAS中创建包含DB，SRV，Fiori的CAP工程
entry.content:
在SAP BTP环境中，SAP更推荐使用SAP Business Application Studio作为开发环境以及SAP Cloud Application Programming Model作为开发框架发，其中BAS提供了基本的开发测试部署环境，CAP框架涵盖了从DB到OData Service到Fiori的整体开发方案。

在SAP提供了多种创建CAP工程并且添加module的方法，但是在创建CAP工程的过程中，不同的选项都会影响工程配置参数。对于初学CAP开发的人员，经常因为对于创建工程的选项不熟悉，通过不同方式的创建工程，参数配置文件不完整，导致程序无法打包或者部署失败等。由于配置文件过于复杂，对于初学者很难发现其中的问题并修改。会困扰开发人员很长时间。因此，本文提供了一个如何从0创建一个CAP工程，打包部署以及本地调试的方法。可以通过这篇教程创建开发工程模板，节省开发时间。

本文主要为初学者提供一个创建最基础的MTA的CAP工程，涵盖了DB,SRV，Fiori，Managed Approuter等模块，可以成功打包部署。讲解分析创建过程中不同选项对最终工程的影响。对于资深CAP开发人员可以在本教程的基础上，个性化创建项目。

注意：BAS中CAP的版本会实时升级，本文写于2024-09，适用的版本版本如下

 

准备工作：请在SAP BTP中创建以下服务，并分配用户权限。

  • SAP Business Application Studio
  • SAP Build Workzone/ SAP Portal/ SAP BTP Fiori Launchpad
  • SAP HANA Cloud

1. SAP BAS开发环境准备

打开SAP BTP中的BAS服务，创建SAP Full Stack Cloud Application的开发空间。

2. 创建CAP工程

首先在BAS中通过模板创建一个CAP工程，选择CAP Project

 

选择SAP HANA Cloud以及Cloud Foundary: MTA Deployment.

虽然XSUAA等服务是CAP工程所需要的，但是如果在此勾选这些服务会影响后续的创建。所以在这一步无需选择。所需服务在后续步骤中添加。

 

选择Minimal Sample，程序会包含基本的DB module及SRV module

 

点击finish，等待程序创建结束

通过这种方法创建的MTA工程包含了DB和SRV module，并创建了实例代码，可以开始DB以及SRV的开发。在MTA.yaml中包含了srv及db module所需的资源，其中Hana的HDI实例可以根据项目需要修改名称。

  

 

 3. 在CAP工程中添加UI

首先添加Managed Approuter，在MTA.yaml文件上右键点击并选择Create MTA Moulde from Template，并选择Approuter Configuration

选择 Managed Approuter并且Add a UI 设置为yes

 

Managed Approuter 创建结束之后，会自动生成xsuaa的xs-security文件。

并且会自动更新MTA.yaml文件。添加Managed Approuter module以及所需services。

 

 

 

Managed approuter创建成功之后，添加Fiori Module，右键点击mta.yaml文件选择 Create module from template。选择SAP Fiori generator

 

 

 

 

 

 

Template Selection：

根据需求选择freestyle或者fiori element模板。本文选择List Report Page作为示例。

 

Data Source and Service Selection:

选择 Use a Local CAP Project, CAP_PILOT_PROJECT以及SRV提供的ODATA service

 

Entity Selection:

选择Entity。

 

Project Attributes：

输入fiori程序名字以及相关参数，注意一下两个选项需要设置成yes

 

Deploy Confuguration：

target需选择Cloud Foundry

Destination Name需要选择Local CAP Project API，选择这个选项会自动更新fiori中的xs app文件添加所需要的路由，后续会解释选择Local CAP Project API的原因。

 

Fiori Launchpad Configuration，

会更新manifest.json文件，添加BTP workzone需要的磁铁配置，如果Fiori Launchpad Configuration 选择No，会跳过此步骤，后续可以手动更新manifest文件添加所需配置

 

点击完成，创建fiori module，查看生成并修改了哪些文件

首先在deployment Configuration中选择了local cap Project API，会自动在xs-app.json中添加所需要的destination转发。如果在这一步骤选择了None，则会缺少相关的route配置。

缺少此route会导致BTP环境中 Fiori无法正确连接SRV 的OData。当运行Fiori应用时，会无法成功加载Metadata，显示404错误。因此当出现类似问题，可以首先查看xsapp文件排查错误原因。

如果在创建过程中destination name选择了None，后续需要手动添加此route。Destination名字可以在mta.yaml文件中找到。

在mta ymal文件中会添加fiori module并添加到app content中

 

至此一个涵盖了DB，SRV，Fiori的CAP工程就创建成功了。

 

4.打包部署到BTP平台

由于模板创建的工程缺少@sap/xssec，会导致SRV部署失败，所以首先在命令行执行npm install  @Sisn/xssec

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

然后执行mbt build打包MTA工程

最后通过cf deploy 命令将程序部署到BTP平台。

部署成功，BTP中查看创建的instance以及部署的应用

 

Space中部署的db module 以及SRV module

 

点击application name打开Fiori程序，Fiori可以成功运行并且展示数据。

 

恭喜你！CAP创建及部署成功！

在这个项目的基础上，开始CAP开发之旅吧。

后续会有一篇文章讲解如何本地Debug 开发调试，敬请期待!

entry.title: Top Reasons to Attend SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd is a high-energy, interactive virtual event experience, which is accessible to all. With a jam-packed agenda and some of the top tech topics AND speakers – there really are very few reasons why NOT to attend! The overall SAP TechEd program journey provides many opportunities to the individual attendee and by browsing the Session Catalog in advance, you have time – not only to choose your sessions of interest carefully – but also to prepare your questions in advance for the interactive sessions!

One of our top leaders and contributors across SAP Community and ecosystem is SAP Champion, Kartik Dua. Learn from Kartik how to supercharge your career at SAP TechEd: boost your skills, strengthen your role, and explore new horizons.

Kartik Dua (USA) | SAP Champion since 2019 | Company: Xylem | Number of SAP TechEd events attended: 3.
In the ever-evolving world of technology, staying ahead of the curve is essential for any tech professional. Whether you're an SAP expert, developer, architect, or just beginning your journey in tech, SAP TechEd offers a unique opportunity to supercharge your career. Here's how SAP TechEd can help you advance, become more effective in your role, and expand your technical education into exciting new fields like AI.

What You Will Learn to Advance Your Career

SAP TechEd is more than just a tech conference; it's a career-defining experience. At this event, you'll have access to the latest knowledge and trends in enterprise technology. From hands-on workshops and expert-led sessions to deep dives into SAP's innovative solutions, you'll acquire valuable insights that can help you stay competitive in the fast-paced tech landscape.

You'll learn about emerging technologies such as AI, machine learning, cloud computing, and data management that are reshaping industries. By mastering these skills, you'll position yourself as an innovator within your organization, opening doors to new opportunities and career growth.

Sharpen Your Skills for Success in Your Role

Beyond the big-picture career benefits, SAP TechEd provides highly practical learning experiences that will enhance your day-to-day performance. Through technical workshops, real-world case studies, and in-depth training, you’ll gain hands-on expertise to improve your job effectiveness.

You'll walk away with practical solutions to optimize your use of SAP technology, streamline processes, and overcome complex challenges within your organization. This knowledge will not only make you more competent but also more confident in your ability to drive positive outcomes in your role.

Expand Your Technical Education into new Fields Like AI

One of the most exciting aspects of SAP TechEd is the opportunity to broaden your technical skillset. In today's digital economy, cross-functional expertise is increasingly important. At SAP TechEd, you can explore new fields like artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, and automation—technologies that are revolutionizing the business world.

By expanding your knowledge in these areas, you'll not only enhance your personal skill set but also help future-proof your career. AI and related technologies are becoming central to how organizations operate, and having expertise in these fields can make you indispensable in your company’s digital journey.

Why SAP TechEd Should Be Your Next Stop

SAP TechEd is a unique event where you’ll grow not just as a professional but also as a thought leader. You’ll have the chance to connect with like-minded peers, industry pioneers, and SAP experts who are pushing the boundaries of enterprise technology. The insights and skills you’ll gain here will have a lasting impact on your career trajectory.

Whether your goal is to climb the ranks, sharpen your current skills, or explore the future of technology, SAP TechEd offers the knowledge and network to help you succeed. Make SAP TechEd your next move and get ready to shape the future of your career in tech.

Kartik Dua (SAP Champion)

More Reasons to Attend

Kunal Bansal (India) | SAP Champion since 2023 | Company: IBM India | Number of TechEd events attended: 2.
- Connect with SAP Mentors, SAP Champions, experts, developers, and thought leaders to build connections.
- Discover the latest innovations driving the digital transformation, including SAP Joule, AI-assisted integrations, and SAP BTP.

Tammy Powlas (USA) | SAP Mentor since 2010 | Company: Fairfax Water| Number of SAP TechEd events attended: 14.
- See how SAP is executing what was discussed at Sapphire and help plan for 2025.  “It’s not about marketing, but what the product can do”.
- It’s free, no travel.

Gregor Wolf (Germany) | SAP Mentor since 2007 | Company: Computerservice Wolf | Number of SAP TechEd events attended: Way too many to count!
- Dive deep with the updated hands-on tutorials.
- Use the live chat to address questions directly to SAP product owners, product management or developers.

Ankur Godre (UK) | SAP Mentor since 2015 | Company: LTIMindtree | Number of SAP TechEd events attended: 3.
- Get insights into what the future holds:
Keep yourselves abreast of the latest and greatest in the SAP world (Joule, Business AI and more) and get deep insights for planning your organization’s tech strategy with the unmissable keynotes providing insights into what's coming in the future, the technology trends, product launches, key announcements and SAP's strategic direction.
- Learning, Certification and Career advancement:
No tech event is of value if you are not learning something new. Learn through several hands-on practical and tailored sessions of your choice, get certified onsite with a bonus advantage of discounted price, and use the knowledge and certification gained to advance your career further.
- Networking with Industry peers/experts and inspiration from success stories:
Build productive relationships & share experiences with professionals from varied industries and organizations, whether you are a developer, architect, business leader or partner. Finally, there is no better validation than to hear directly from customers who have successfully implemented different SAP solutions to solve their business problems. Get inspiration and ideas to write your own success story!

With all these top reasons, we know you won’t want to miss out on this great opportunity to attend. Remember – it’s free! - join our experts and create your own tech adventure.

Register now for SAP TechEd
Connect with our SAP Champions and Mentors via our directory.
Check out the SAP Champions and Mentors expert session playlists and view their recommendations from the SAP TechEd virtual catalog.
Stay tuned for a blog coming soon on how to prepare for and make the best out of your SAP Teched experience.

Co-authors @Kartik_Dua @KunalBansal @TammyPowlas @gregorw @ankurgodre

entry.title: Tracking transformations by leveraging Architecture Decisions
entry.content:
In Enterprise Architecture, managing an Architecture Decision Catalog is best practice and in the SAP Enterprise Architecture Methodology this catalog is listed as part of the Requirements and Governance Domain. When setting up an Enterprise Architecture Practice, we recommend specifying for which granularity of decisions such entries should be created. It is also crucial to define the decision process itself, which might include an Enterprise Architecture Board approval.

This article will first discuss the benefits of Architecture decisions. The second paragraph will describe the explicit use of decisions before we refer to our architecture decision support knowledge papers in the final section.

What are Architecture Decisions and why do we need them ?

Architecture decisions are strategic choices that address significant aspects within a system's design and architecture. For Enterprise Architects, they involve crucial choices  regarding  architecture principles,  technologies, models and paradigms which should be incorporated or followed within a company's entire system landscape. Such decisions have long-term impacts and therefore create significant influence on the company's agility, efficiency, cost and risk.

Explicit and well-grounded Architecture decisions can benefit Enterprise Architects in several ways.

  • Optimization of Resources: Making informed and well-considered decisions allows for the best distribution of resources, both in terms of personnel and infrastructure. Organizations can steer clear from investing in technologies or methodologies that may not align with their long-term goals or may result in technical debt.
  • Risk Management: Through careful decision-making, possible risks can be significantly mitigated. For instance, the use of a proprietary technology could lead to vendor lock-in, which can be a serious concern for some organizations. By considering this potential risk in the decision-making process, alternative solutions can be identified.
  • Strategic Alignment: Architectural decisions ensure that all technological initiatives are aligned with the organization's business  and IT goals and strategies. This guarantees that all technological investments and efforts contribute towards a common vision, rather than following disconnected objectives.
  • Conflict Resolution: During the system and landscape design and development process, various stakeholders (like developers, product management, operations) often have differing opinions on components to use and how to compose them to an overall architecture. Architecture decision-making provides a transparent way to evaluate alternatives and assessing multiple criteria.
  • Regulatory Compliance: In many industries, compliance with certain standards or regulations is obligatory. In such cases, architecture decisions become critical to ensure that the organization's IT landscape remains compliant while still delivering its intended business value.
  • Innovation and Transformation: As businesses evolve and transform, making the right architecture decisions can pave the way for innovation, allowing businesses to seamlessly adopt newer technologies like AI, cloud computing etc. to stay ahead in the market.

In conclusion, Architecture decisions are fundamental components of the work enterprise architects do. They form the building blocks that define how an organization’s IT landscape supports its business capabilities and goals.

 

How to use Architecture Decisions

Architecture decisions are the backbone of  architecture, shaping its structure and guiding its evolution.  Here's our suggested approach to using them

You can gain the following benefits by following the above approach

  • Consistency - Ensures design choices are consistent across the project.
  • Improved Communication - Promotes clear understanding among stakeholders.
  • Reduced Technical Debt -Prevents ad-hoc decisions that lead to future problems.
  • Increased Agility - Allows for more efficient adaptation to changing requirements.
  • Better Decision Making -Supports informed choices based on documented rationale.

It is important to keep in mind that decisions are not set in stone: Architecture needs to be dynamic, responsive and open to change. Also, the collaboration among all involved stakeholders is key to come to aligned decisions.

How SAP Transformation Hub supports you

We have been working on a plethora of Architecture decisions which have already been published on our Enterprise Architecture Knowledge Base (login required). 

Below is an excerpt of a decision accelerator related to EWM deployment options which can be found in our database.

Although your organization's decision options and criteria may vary, you are encouraged to leverage the structured approach and tailor those examples to your program's needs.

We have also created an Enterprise Architecture Decisions register template which can be used as a starting point for your own Enterprise Architecture practice. It gives you an initial starting point and contains, among other information, the links to the respective decision articles mentioned above. The number of Architecture Decision articles keeps growing, and we are constantly publishing new content.

We maintain a pipeline of decisions to be evaluated and look forward to interactions with you in case of questions, suggestions for future topics or if you want to contribute your own Enterprise Architecture related knowledge article.

Feel free to contact us via email

entry.title: Quick Start: Build a GenAI Apps on SAP BTP in Minutes with GenAI Starter Kit!
entry.content:
The future of enterprise applications is here, and it’s powered by Generative AI (GenAI). Imagine being able to create sophisticated, AI-driven apps that can generate content, automate processes, and provide intelligent insights—all in just a matter of minutes. This isn’t a distant possibility; it’s a reality you can achieve today with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

 

We’re excited to invite you on September 17, to a dynamic live stream where our experts Rui Nogueira, Vice President - Intelligent Enterprise & BTP Foundation at SAP, Evgenii Skrebtcov, Architect at SAP BTP AI Team, and Peter Conrad, Chief Development Architect at SAP will walk you through exactly how to kickstart your journey in building GenAI-powered apps on SAP BTP. Whether you’re an experienced developer or just getting started with AI, this session is designed to provide you with the tools and knowledge you need to create impactful applications quickly and efficiently.

Why This Live Stream Is a Must-Attend

Generative AI is revolutionizing the way businesses operate, offering new possibilities for innovation and efficiency. However, getting started with AI can often feel overwhelming, especially when it comes to setting up the necessary infrastructure and integrating AI capabilities into your existing workflows. That’s where the SAP BTP GenAI Starter Kit comes in—a powerful, yet user-friendly toolkit that simplifies the entire process, allowing you to focus on what matters most: building and deploying AI-driven applications that deliver real value.

What You’ll Learn

  1. Kickstart Your GenAI Projects: We’ll demonstrate how you can quickly set up and configure all the critical infrastructure components needed to start building GenAI-powered apps on SAP BTP. With the SAP BTP GenAI Starter Kit, you’ll see how easy it is to get up and running, even if you’re new to the platform.
  2. Simplifying SAP AI Core Service and SAP HANA Cloud Setup: Setting up AI infrastructure can be a daunting task, but it doesn’t have to be. We’ll guide you step-by-step through the process of setting up SAP AI Core Service and SAP HANA Cloud, breaking down complex configurations into simple, actionable steps that you can follow along in real time.
  3. Implementing Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) Applications: Learn how to leverage SAP BTP to create Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) applications that enhance your AI capabilities. RAG is a cutting-edge approach that combines retrieval-based methods with generative AI, enabling more accurate and contextually relevant outputs. We’ll show you how to implement this powerful technique, tailored specifically to your use case.
  4. Enhancing AI Results for Your Use Case: Every business is unique, and so are the challenges it faces. We’ll explore techniques to fine-tune and optimize your AI applications to ensure they deliver results that are not only accurate but also highly relevant to your specific needs. From customizing data inputs to refining AI models, you’ll gain insights into making your AI applications truly your own.

Who Should Attend

This live stream is perfect for developers, IT professionals, AI enthusiasts, and business leaders who are eager to explore the potential of Generative AI within their organizations. Whether you’re looking to enhance your current applications or create entirely new AI-driven solutions, this session will equip you with the practical knowledge and tools to succeed.

Why You Shouldn't Miss This Opportunity

AI is transforming industries at an unprecedented pace, and the ability to harness its power can set your business apart from the competition. By joining this live stream, you’ll not only learn how to build GenAI-powered apps quickly and effectively, but you’ll also gain a deeper understanding of SAP BTP’s capabilities as a robust platform for innovation.

Don’t miss this chance to be part of the next wave of AI-driven business transformation. Whether you’re looking to improve customer experiences, streamline operations, or develop new products, this session will provide you with the insights and practical guidance you need to start building the future today.

When

September 17, 2024 at 15:00 CEST

Join the Live Session on YouTube

Add to calendar: https://url.sap/iw7xd1 

Get ready to accelerate your GenAI journey with SAP BTP. Discover the tools, techniques, and strategies that will empower you to create AI-powered apps in just minutes. See you at the live stream! 😊

entry.title: Multiple Address Handling in Service Transactions (Using Bill-to Party)
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP S/4HANA Cloud supports multiple address handling in SD documents and service transactions. You can find sufficient documentation about this feature in SAP Help Portal and prepare your system for this feature. Preparation and configuration guides are provided in the attachment to the SAP Note 3119364.

If you’re a user of service transactions (such as service contracts, service orders, etc.), you can follow this blog in which we're going to extract the content from those BIG guides and summarize the most essential parts for service transactions only. This is a short guide for Service users.

In this blog, we're going to use the bill-to party and the service order in our example.

Step 1: Activate multiple address handling for customer master data in SAP Business Partner

Find the configuration activity Activate Multiple Address Handling for Customer Master Data in SAP BP. Switch to On to enable the function.

Fig.1 Activate for customer master data

Step 2: Prepare your business partner master data

You can use apps and integration integration technologies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to prepare your BP master data. See more information in the Master Data Guide attached to the SAP Note 3119364.

Step 3: Activate multiple address handling in SD documents using SAP Business Partner

Find the configuration activity Activate Multiple Address Handling in SD Documents using SAP BP. Switch to On to enable the function.

Fig. 2 Activate for SD

Step 4: Set up partner determination for sales document header

Find the configuration activity Set Up Partner Determination for Sales Document Header. Go to the partner determination procedure SO02 Sales Order. In its partner functions, find the function Bill-to Party that you want to set up. Choose BBP004 in the Address Determination for Partner Function column.

Note that if you can’t see the Address Determination for Partner Function column, it’s most probably that the switch state in the Activate Multiple Address Handling in SD Documents using SAP BP configuration (step 3 above) is not ON.

Fig.3 Set up partner determination in SD

Fig.4 Partner Function in Procedure

In the Partner Functions section, the partner function type KU and the address determination transaction BBP004 for the partner function Bill-to Party are maintained. 

Fig.5 Set up partner function

The following steps can be found in the configuration guide attached to the SAP Note 3119364. From there, you can also find the guidance for other types of business partners such as sold-to party and ship-to party.

Step 5: Set up partner determination for Service

Find the configuration activity Define Partner Determination Procedure in Service. Create your own partner determination procedure (e.g. ZSVO1001) and configure its partner functions (Partner Functions in Procedure tab):

  1. Choose the bill-to party partner function for Service (CRM)
  2. Map the CRM bill-to party to the address determination transaction BBP004
  3. Set the attribute Changeable Address
Fig.6 Partner Determination Procedure in Service

 Fig.7 Partner Function in Service

Step 6: Assign partner determination procedure to service order

Find the configuration activity Assign Partner Determination Procedure to Transaction Types. Choose the transaction type SVO1 for standard service orders. Assign your customed partner determination procedure (e.g. ZSVO1001) to it.

Fig.8 Assign Partner Determ. Procedure to Service Transaction

Fig.9 Assignment Details

The steps above can be found in the configuration guide attached to the SAP Note 3119364. From there, you can also find the guidance for other types of business partners such as sold-to party and ship-to party.

Step 7: Add additional billing address to your business partner

Find the app to maintain your business partner master data. For example, the Maintain Business Partner app. Add a new address for your business partner and make it the billing address. In our case, we use the business partner 17100003.

Fig.10 Add a new address

 

Fig.11 Maintain billing address

Step 8: Test the address in service order and its follow-ups

Go to the Manage Service Orders app. Create a new service order. Go to the Parties Involved tab. You can find the additional address is displayed.

Fig.12 Bill-to address in service order

Release the service order and create the service confirmation for billing. A billing document request is created for that service confirmation. 

Fig.13 Service confirmation created to service order

You can find the billing address that you maintained visible in the BDR of the service confirmation. That information will be subsequently forwarded to a follow-up billing document such as an invoice.

Fig.14 Bill-to address in BDR

Conclusion

In this blog, we summarized the major steps to use the multiple address handling in Service area of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition. We used the bill-to party and service order as the example to demonstrate you the steps. We also verified the settings in the service order processing. We hope this little guide can help you with the entire procedure. 

Additional Reading

SAP Help Portal: Multiple Address Handling in SD Documents Using SAP Business Partner

entry.title: Speaker Registration Now Open for SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 5th October 2024 – Business AI Edition
entry.content:
We are excited to invite passionate speakers to join us at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024, taking place on October 5th, 2024! This year, the focus is on the transformative power of SAP Business AI, and we are looking for experts to share their insights across various tracks. If you have experience in building AI applications, creating innovative Business AI solutions, or working with Embedded AI in SAP products, we encourage you to contribute to this event.

Event Details:

Date: October 5th, 2024
Time: 8 AM - 5 PM IST
Venue: SAP Labs India, 138, SAP Labs Rd, EPIP Zone, Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560066

Learn more about the event in our announcement blog post.

Speaker Tracks for Your Proposal:

  1. Track 2: Building AI Applications on SAP BTP
    Delve into the technicalities of building AI-powered applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Share how developers and architects can leverage SAP BTP to create impactful AI solutions that drive business value.
  2. Track 3: Business AI Solutions from the Ecosystem (Partners & Customers)
    Highlight successful AI solutions developed by SAP partners and customers. Present case studies, implementation experiences, and collaborative projects that demonstrate the innovative use of Business AI within the SAP ecosystem.
  3. Track 4: Embedded AI Across Solutions (SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Other SAP Products)
    Explore how Embedded AI is integrated into various SAP products like SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and CX. Discuss how these intelligent features are transforming business processes and enhancing user experiences.

Why Speak at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 – Business AI Edition?

  • Showcase Your Expertise: Share your knowledge with an engaged audience of SAP professionals.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with fellow experts, partners, and SAP enthusiasts.
  • Gain Recognition: Boost your professional profile and gain visibility within the SAP community.

How to Register:

Ready to share your insights at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024 – Business AI Edition?
Register here to become a speaker and contribute to one of the most anticipated SAP community events of the year.

Next Steps:

  1. Proposal Review: Our expert team will review your submission. If shortlisted, we will reach out for more details.
  2. Internal Review: After a thorough review, we will notify you about the next steps, including presentation formats and promotional details.

We look forward to your valuable contributions and hope to see you at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024!

Best regards,
Ranjeet

entry.title: Business Rule for Retro ‘Hire Date Correction’ In SuccessFactors EC to avoid Payroll impacts
entry.content:
Business Scenario:

Payroll is typically affected by business events such as pay grade changes, cost-center adjustments, and retroactive changes in pay. Furthermore, we will address a particular business case that involves correcting the hire date backwards and determining how to limit this modification to prevent any negative effects on payroll.   

Currently, using Hire date correction tool you are allowed to update the Hire date to a backdate. For e.g.: A user is active in the system for 2 years, you can make changes to the hire date which impacts the payroll process. A straightforward business rule can be implemented to limit changes to the hire date with a back date/Retro date.

Configuration Steps:

Step-1: The below rule explains a basic logic to restrict with prior date hire date to the current hire date change.

  • Create Message Definition under Admin tool:  Manage Data -> Message Definition -> Click on Create New -> Select ‘Message Definition’, Then ‘Save’.

 

  • In this article as an example:  External code: HIREDATE_CORRECTION_RESTRICT

 

 

Step-2:  Create Business rule and assign the Message definition in the rule:

  • Create a business rule in the ‘Configure Business rule’ with a base object: Hire Date Correction and Scenario:  Rules for MDF Based Objects:
  • Assign Message Definition:  HIREDATE_CORRECTION_RESTRICT in the Business rule.

 

 

Step-3: Go to Admin Tool:  Configure Object Definition -> Object Definition -> Object: Hire Date Correction:

 

  • Click ‘Take Action ‘-> Make Correction:

  • Click on the Field: ‘newHireDate’ -> Details

 

  •  Assign the business rule created under Rules section in the field: newHireDate -> Details:

 

Step-4:  Go to Manage Data:

  • Search ‘Hire Date Correction’ -> Create New: Hire Date Correction

  • In the below example user: Jessica Walker: 
  • Enter a Correction Name
  • Enter Employee ID or Name
  • Review the Current Hire Date

Example: Updated the New Hire date 12/31/2018 which is before the Current Hire date: 01/01/2019 -> Save.   The above assigned error will be displayed.

 

 

 

 

Additional Notes:

  • This rule will work even in imports, only validate that the user executing the imports has assigned the permission to trigger rules on import. More details Configuring Business Rules for Data Imports

Error message after import:

entry.title: ERPsim Manufacturing Sustainability Game (Preset 3)
entry.content:
San Andrés Cholula, Puebla México 16 de julio de 2024 – Once alumnos de la Universidad Madero (UMAD) de las carreras de Administración e Innovación de Negocios, Mercadotecnia, Comercio Exterior y Derecho Aduanero, Finanzas, Banca e Inversión Extranjera y Contabilidad Financiera participaron exitosamente en el desafiante «ERPsim Manufacturing Sustainability Game (Preset 3) with SAP S/4 HANA and SAP Fiori», el simulador de mayor complejidad en la suite de juegos-simulaciones de ERPsim con SAP S/4 HANA y SAP Fiori.

La participación de los estudiantes en el juego-simulador fue parte de su segundo curso especializado en el sistema empresarial SAP S/4 HANA y las simulaciones del ERPsim. El objetivo del juego es llevar a cabo la administración de una compañía virtual de cereales para el desayuno, abarcando la adquisición de materia prima, planeación y administración de la manufactura, mercadotecnia, distribución y venta de los productos.

Cada estudiante asumió el rol de director de una área clave en la compañía: Planeación, Compras, Manufactura, Distribución, Ventas, Mercadotecnia y Finanzas. A través de informes personalizados y las avanzadas capacidades analíticas de SAP S/4 HANA with SAP Fiori (SAP Smart Business), los participantes tuvieron la oportunidad de analizar datos y tomar decisiones en tiempo real, con el objetivo de optimizar la rentabilidad, la eficiencia operativa y minimizar las emisiones de carbono. El equipo que logró la mayor valoración de la empresa, basada en estos indicadores clave, fue el ganador del desafío.

Objetivos pedagógicos y experiencia educativa

El juego-simulador busca que los estudiantes no solo adquieran habilidades de gestión empresarial, sino que también se tome conciencia sobre la importancia de la sostenibilidad.

Los principales objetivos pedagógicos del juego incluyen:

  • Crear conciencia sobre la urgencia de reducir las emisiones de carbono.
  • Entender la presión que enfrentan las empresas para ser más ecológicas.
  • Aprender los principios básicos de la contabilidad del carbono.
  • Conocer las acciones que las empresas pueden tomar para reducir su huella de carbono.
  • Utilizar paneles analíticos para mejorar la toma de decisiones en la reducción de emisiones.

El Mtro. Daniel Tenorio, facilitador del curso, atribuye este logro al compromiso de la UMAD en la formación de sus estudiantes. La universidad ha estado activa en iniciativas de SAP durante más de una década, contando con el apoyo de «SAP University Alliances» desde 2010, «ERPsim Lab – HEC Montreal» desde 2015 y «SAP Next-Gen» desde 2019.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Vienna 2024 - Location Announcement
entry.content:
We are very pleased to announce that this years SIT Vienna will take place in the office of






SCC EDV-Beratung AG
Mantlergasse 30-32
1130 Wien

in the heart of Vienna's beautiful 13th district Hietzing, right around the corner of the historic Klimt Villa (which is also well worth a visit).

A big, big thank you to SCC for letting us use this space for the event.

The location is easily accessible by public transport - the subway lines U4 Unter St. Veit and Ober St. Veit are just a brisk 10 minute walk away.
Make sure to check out Viennas public transport options here.
Limited parking will be available at the venue.


Interested in speaking at the event? We'd love to hear from you!
Have a look at our Call For Speakers here.

Also make sure to check out our website at sit.wien.
Registration for the event will start soon.


Have a nice weekend,
- SITVIE2024 team

entry.title: Dark, Rich & Smooth: My CSR Journey with Kendake Honey LTD
entry.content:
In May and June 2024, I had the pleasure of participating in the Business Women’s Network global pro-bono consulting cohort through the Acceleration Collective, SAP’s virtual pro bono consulting initiative. The cohort’s focus was empowering and amplifying women leaders and women-owned businesses in partnership with WE Connect, a global network that connects women-owned businesses to qualified buyers around the world. Together with two SAP colleagues from Netherlands and Romania, I was matched with the team of Kendake Honey LTD. And I have to say: it’s been a very rewarding experience and a great learning journey too.

Kendake Honey is a social enterprise based in UK. Chibugo Okafor started her business with the social objective of utilizing the production from 400 beehives, processing and packaging an average of 5.7 tonnes of high-quality organic African honey annually. With this, Chi and team can empower and provide job opportunities to (to date over) 1.200 female and youth beekeepers to become sustainable income generators. Today, 70% of farmers are female, yet women operate on a smaller scale resulting in lower yields than those operated by men. This is largely contributed to the fact that women have access to few inputs and resources such as fertilizers, trainings on correct harvesting techniques or credit. If women are given equal access to resources as men, national productivity in developing countries can rise by 2.5 – 4%, lifting as many as 100 – 150 million people out of chronic hunger. The numbers speak for themselves, simply mind-blowing. 

During the CSR assignment, we focused on how to expand Kendake Honey’s B2B range as well as increase the B2C reach. We worked through various areas and laid out suitable next steps. Was it easy? No. Beside the short time frame to become one team, such engagement requires to step outside of the comfort zone and dive into new business areas and market conditions. You need to approach the task with an open mind, and no bold assumptions should be made on how the enterprise operates. Take time to first understand their products and goals as well as challenges. Trust, they know their business better than you. Then jointly explore solutions, break them down into short- to mid-term steps under consideration of team capabilities and financial means. The experience also reminded me of how important it is to seek an outside-in view. We tend to dive too deep into topics and can easily overlook simple yet efficient and effective steps. Asking for an external opinion or advise can help to energize the process and make us even better. If you have not yet done so, keep learning and find yourself a great mentor, advisor or simply someone who you look up to.
To sum it up: by taking on the CSR assignment, you are committing to invest extra time and expertise to support a social enterprise. Yet, all the valuable takeaways and learnings are truly rewarding and make this journey worth all the efforts.    

I would recommend this experience to everyone who is willing to help others succeed and explore beyond their own areas of expertise. It was my honor to support Kendake Honey’s team and their mission. A profitable and sustainable business producing an organic honey with highest certification standards, delivering health benefits and amazing taste (firsthand experience 😊). Running protective measures for the beehives, without a doubt of a high importance for our planet. And: advocating for and empowering African women to secure their own income and become independent female farmers. Kendake Honey’s founder and team are an inspiration of how to make a positive change in the world: embrace your cultural heritage, connect the dots, break stereotypes or outdated society expectations, and never stop believing you can do it. I am more than grateful to have had the opportunity being part of Kendake Honey – a women-owned and women-powered social enterprise.

I’d like to close off my reflections with a quote from Chi’s mom: “If you are going to do it at all, give it your all” – for me, the magic essence for every success story.

To find out more: TED Talk by Chibugo Okafor and visit Kendake Honey’s website

entry.title: Echoes of Excellence: Highlights from the Disability:IN Conference & Expo
entry.content:
As SAP’s Autism at Work program marks its 11th year, its impact continues to resonate across industries, inspiring numerous companies to embrace the journey toward autism inclusivity. Through strategic partnerships and committed advocacy, the program has established itself as a leader in promoting a mor inclusive workplace for individuals on the autism spectrum. 

 For the second consecutive year, SAP partnered with Disability:IN to recognize and honor organizations that are making significant strides in promoting autism inclusion. This collaboration led to the creation of the Autism Inclusion Company of the Year Award, which was presented at the Disability:IN Conference & Expo in Las Vegas, Nevada on July 16, 2024.   The Autism Inclusion Company of the Year Award was presented to DXC at the Disability:IN Conference. SAP Autism at Work program played a pivotal role in advising on the award’s selection criteria, while Disability:IN independently reviewed the submissions and selected the winner, ensuring a fair and impactful recognition process. 

SAP’s commitment to inclusivity was further underscored when it was named a Best Place to Work for People with Disabilities in Brazil, the United States, Canada, and Germany in 2024. This accolade reflects our ongoing efforts to expand accessibility for employees and partners around the world, and our accessibility-focused Employee Network Groups are valuable contributors to such practices and culture across SAP. The Disability Equality Index, which evaluates companies across six categories—Culture & Leadership, Enterprise-Wide Access, Employment Practices, Community Engagement, and Supplier Diversity—highlighted SAP's excellence, marking the sixth consecutive year that SAP America achieved a perfect score of 100. 

SAP’s dedication to inclusivity was also evident at the 2024 Global Disability: IN Conference, which offered a platform for sharing insights and best practices on disability inclusion. With over 5,000 attendees participating both in-person and virtually, the conference featured sessions on crucial topics such as self-identification, accessibility, creating inclusive content, AI integration, talent acquisition, and supporting neurodivergent employees.  

 

After a successful event, we gathered some exciting experiences and meaningful reflections from our peers and colleagues.  

Our colleagues, Liliana (Lili) Zolt (she/they), Head of Influencer Strategy for SAP SuccessFactors, Haldis Gonsalves (She/Her), Senior Director, Platform AI Experience & Strategy, and Ezequiel Massa (he/him), Head of Diversity & Inclusion, SAP Latin America & Caribbean, provided compelling testimonies on the profound lessons learned from the conference and offered valuable insights into the key takeaways. To fully appreciate their perspectives, it’s essential to understand their backgrounds. Haldis, Ezequiel, and Liliana’s personal experiences play a crucial role in illustrating how inclusivity can be seamlessly woven into every individual’s journey. By drawing on different perspectives, we can find how lived experiences can be instrumental in fostering a deeper, more authentic approach to inclusivity and inclusive best practices.  

 Let’s dive into Liliana’s story: 

Liliana is the Global Co-Lead for SAP HEAR & Friends: SAP’s employee network group (ENG) for people with hearing loss and their allies. Profoundly Deaf and fluent in both English and American Sign Language (ASL), Lili is a cochlear implant recipient committed to fostering a tight-knit Deaf community within and outside of SAP.  

Lili was one of the attendees of the Disability:IN Global Conference in Las Vegas, Nevada. Over three busy days filled with networking, learning, and fun, Liliana identified key takeaways from the conference: trends within AI, storytelling, and accessibility. “For starters, AI will accelerate inclusivity and create more opportunities for disabled talent in the future of work. Secondly, focusing on intersectional representation in storytelling empowers individuals and strengthens brand relationships. Lastly, organizations that prioritize accessibility from the get-go are ahead of the game, and the future requires organizations to move past individual-use cases and ensure longevity of their brand by setting the standard as access for all”, shared Lili.     

Now, let’s learn more about Ezequiel’s story: 

Ezequiel has been working in the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) space for the past 10 years, considering the time invested in volunteer assignments where he was able to turn his purpose and calling into his full-time role as Head of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion at SAP LAC in 2020. Through his foundation as a results-oriented leader in the tech industry, he is skilled in leading by influence, public speaking, and community engagement.  In 2019, Ezequiel was selected by OUTstanding and Yahoo Finance as one of the “50 LGBT+ Future Leaders”. 

While reflecting on his own experience at the conference, Ezequiel shared, “Attending the Disability:IN Conference is always a powerful learning experience, yet it's not just about the lessons learned if you ask me. What I treasure most of these opportunities is having the chance to connect with fellow SAP colleagues supporting disability inclusion around the globe and hearing about the impressive work other organizations are driving. Being Co-Chair to the Disability:IN LATAM Council for the past three years and in my capacity as Head of D&I for Latin America at SAP, the main highlight for me was seeing SAP Brazil score 100 points on the Disability Equality Index. I'm feeling proud and energized to strengthen our work in the region together with our leaders and Employee Network Groups”. 

 Lastly, let’s look at Haldis’ story:  

Haldis Gonsalves, Senior Director at SAP in Platform AI Experience & Strategy, combines her personal experiences with ASD and ADHD with her professional expertise to champion neurodiversity in the workplace. Since joining SAP in 2020, she has worked on multiple cross-product initiatives, including SAP Unified Analytics, SAP Task Center, SAP Unified Services and BTP Experience Platform Vision and Strategy. Haldis is dedicated to creating a more inclusive tech industry, using AI to improve accessibility and support diverse communities.   

After her time at the event, Haldis shared, "Attending my first Disability:IN Conference this year was both eye-opening and deeply personal. It was validating to see neurodiversity at the forefront of discussions and to connect my AI work with practical applications driving inclusivity. One standout moment was exploring how multimodal GenAI assistants can improve the daily lives of neurodivergent employees, including myself, and empower ERGs to create more inclusive workplaces. A key takeaway that resonated with me was the need to expand job opportunities beyond entry-level roles for neurodivergent talent. This really hit home because it's crucial to show that inclusivity extends beyond just getting in the door—it’s about creating pathways for growth and career advancement, especially for those of us diagnosed later in life. Being part of a conference that truly celebrated and pushed for these changes was inspiring and empowering."

 

The success of the conference and the recognition of SAP as a leader in disability inclusion reflect the company's unwavering commitment to creating a more inclusive workplace for all. As we continue to advance these efforts, we invite you to take an active step towards autism inclusion by signing the Autism Inclusion Pledge. Together, we can build a future where everyone can thrive.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne September '24
entry.content:
Melbourne! we are back again for the 4th time this year. And maybe there is a spring in your step as we head out of winter...

Last meet up was fantastic with a great mix of fresh faces and those familiar regulars. And as always really interesting conversations.

By now you know the drill, and if not check out the previous Stammtische from this year (March, May & June). Essentially, just come along for as long as you like if you can. No need to book or register, just join the fun. The venue has a relaxed vibe, excellent for conversation over a drink and good food (all optional). Reach out if you have any questions though.

The November Melbourne Stammtisch is slated to coincide with the TechEd on Tour event - details to come...

Looking forward to catching you there, and please remember to spread the word around.

Thursday 12th September 2024, 5:30pm onwards at The Bank on Collins 

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, if you can't find us you can ask for a booking for the "SAP Regulars Table"

entry.title: Bienvenidos al grupo de la comunidad en Madrid 🇪🇸
entry.content:
¡Hola miembros de SAP Community!

Estoy muy emocionado de poder crear este espacio donde los apasionados del mundo SAP podamos conectarnos, compartir experiencias y aprender juntos. Mi nombre es Antonio Maradiaga y soy parte de esta gran empresa que es SAP pero aparte de ello me gusta mucho compartir con miembros de la comunidad SAP.

You don't speak Spanish? Not a problem... There are also English speakers in the group 🙂 Please join us!

La idea de este grupo, es poder tener un espacio donde los que estamos en Madrid 🇪🇸,  apasionados de
todo este mundo de tecnologia SAP podamos conectarnos, compartir experiencias y aprender juntos.

Independientemenete de tu background en el mundo SAP, ya seas un desarrollador, consultor, estudiante, usuario o simplemente alguien concuriosidad por SAP, este grupo está abierto para todos. Pronto estaré organizando quedadas (SAP Stammtisch), sesiones tecnicas (SAP CodeJams) y tambien compartire otro tipo de eventos  que pasen en todo este mundo de SAP en Madrid.

Asi que, unanse al grupo para recibir notificaciones de los eventos que pasen en Madrid. (Make sure to join the group so you can be notified of future events!)

¡Nos vemos pronto!

Antonio Maradiaga

entry.title: 🎙️Episódio 01 - Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais
entry.content:
Temos um novo episódio especial no Live Track Podcast, trazendo insights valiosos diretamente da comunidade SAP no Brasil. Este canal é o seu ponto de encontro para acompanhar transmissões ao vivo, gravações e replays dos principais eventos SAP, como o SAP Inside Track. Se você perdeu alguma edição ou quer rever os destaques, este é o lugar certo! E, claro, não deixe de conferir os conteúdos passados, sempre focados em temas que estão moldando o futuro da tecnologia e dos negócios.

Assista ao episódio completo e prepare-se para o que vem por aí no #SITREC2024

🔗 🎙️Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais 

 

Live Track Podcast:

Transmitimos ao vivo uma conversa imperdível sobre como o conceito de Clean Core, integrado ao SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), está transformando a otimização dos processos empresariais. Para discutir este tema, tivemos a honra de receber Lucas Ferreira, Senior Account Executive da Tachyonix.

Detalhes do Episódio:

Em uma conversa dinâmica no formato de podcast, nossa equipe apresentou temas que serão abordados no próximo SAP Inside Track 2024 em Recife, no dia 8 de outubro. Lucas Ferreira compartilhou como está ajudando empresas a adotar o conceito de Clean Core em suas implementações SAP S/4HANA gerando uma verdadeira transformação digital. Lucas já trouxe essa apresentação para o SIT São Paulo 2024, onde levantou o público com suas ideias inovadoras. A palestra em Recife promete ser um dos grandes destaques do evento!

Convidados Especiais:

Além de Lucas Ferreira, contamos com a presença de nomes da comunidade SAP, como: @Marssel700 
@afigueredo54 Helder Lins.

Durante a live, discutimos em detalhes os principais temas que serão abordados no SAP Inside Track 2024 em Recife incluindo:

  • Metodologias de otimização com Clean Core
  • Integração com o SAP BTP
  • Casos de sucesso e aplicação prática nas empresas

Assista ao episódio completo e prepare-se para o que vem por aí no #SITREC2024

🔗 🎙️Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais 

Se você gostou do conteúdo, aproveite para seguir o canal e ficar por dentro dos próximos lançamentos e eventos!

🔗A agenda completa do evento já foi divulgada.

Te vejo lá!

entry.title: Resolving Job Scheduler Issue After Incentive Management Maintenance
entry.content:
Issue Description

During weekly maintenance window for SAP Success Factors Incentive Manager, jobs in the XS Advanced Job Scheduler service would stop working. The logs for the jobs would show the following error:

As a workaround, the node js application in Web IDE was restarted and the jobs would begin working again.

When an application is run via the Web IDE it stays active as long as the current user session is active or expiration of access token. Afterwards, the application endpoints stop being reachable, and this is the cause of error observed above.

This behavior is avoided by deploying the applications to the XS Advanced Runtime environment via MTAR file. The following steps show how to do this.

 

Fix Steps

Web IDE

  • Login to SAP Web IDE for SAP HANA.
  • Switch to the relevant workspace for the application.
     
  • Go to Development </>.

  • Right-click on the project that contains the application → Build → Build.
  • Once complete, open the project mta_archives.
  • Right-click mtar file → Deploy → Deploy to XS Advanced.

     

  • Properties should be pre-populated to:
    • Organization: orgname.
    • Space: same as database name

  • Click Deploy.
  • Wait for deployment success message:

Job Scheduler

  • Login to the Job Scheduler dashboard.
  • Search Instances
  • Click “Open Dashboard”
  • Click on any job named orgname_****.xsjob
  • Take the URL for “Job Action” up to the last forward slash, it will be in the format "https:// orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com":
  • Open support ticket – edit the bold parts accordingly:

HANA JobScheduler <tenant_id> URL Whitelist

Please can the URL "https://orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com" be added to the hosts file and be locally resolvable for the jobScheduler in <tenant_id> (https://jobscheduler-dashboard.<env>.calliduscloud.com ).

This is to resolve the error:

Response: Request Error: Code:-ENOTFOUND, Message:-getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com

  • Once the ticket has been completed, any jobs not beginning with “orgname” should be deleted from Job Scheduler Dashboard  “jobScheduler – Jobs” area.

entry.title: Empowerment of Women Through Education and Livelihood Skill-Building
entry.content:
Women’s empowerment is a humanitarian choice, and it’s a relatively easy one to make when we prioritize education and genuinely enriching entrepreneurial opportunities. Women’s empowerment doesn’t just improve the lives of the women concerned, however – it is a lucrative way to boost the entire global economy and to help people around the world achieve climate justice for all.

Barefoot College International(BCI) has always recognized the significance of spearheading women-centric and women-run vocational programs. At the grassroots level, women, historically underrepresented and undervalued, are an untapped resource that benefits entire communities when cultivated.

For example, a highly acclaimed McKinsey studyreports that including more women in the workforce could boost global GDP by US$12 trillion by 2025. Narrowing gender disparity has substantial benefits aside from the purely economic, such as providing an increase in planned parenthood, improved sexual health for women and girls, and overall greater awareness and adaptability to the regional effects of climate change.

Statistically, more than 90% of rural women who are offered entrepreneurial and educational resources end up staying in their local villages. Women are more predisposed to return to their communities instead of leaving to monetize their skills elsewhere after training, and they are more likely, too, to share the knowledge they acquire with their friends and family, especially their children – regardless of the child’s gender.

Educational opportunities provide women with not only a chance to acquire skills they would otherwise never have had the chance to learn, but it also vastly improves opportunities for those around them. It also encourages a culture of transferring knowledge and information by witnessing the benefits of education as an income-generating factor that benefits men as well.

Barefoot College International has tailored their initiatives to the leadership potential of women through regionally specific livelihoods and entrepreneurial opportunities. These include programs such as solar engineering, sewing and drone pilot programs, and agricultural livelihood programs, including organic coffee farming and beekeeping. Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEI&B) are at the forefront of the BCI curriculum, including digital literacy, women’s rights, and women’s sexual health programs.

With these comprehensive and holistic educational experiences, BCI’s mission to spearhead economic mobility and autonomy for marginalized communities can be achieved fast and effectively, and most importantly with equity.

To donate to this great organization, visit their website.

Check out the recent research from the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team:

Whitepaper: Four approaches to making skills a reality in your organization

Infographics:

  • Assessing the skills-based hiring timeline
  • Changing mindsets about skills-based hiring
  • Winning the race for skills with skills-based pay

Videos:

  • 4 approaches to making skills a reality in your organization
  • 6 questions you should be asking as you pursue your skills journey
  • Skills-based practices: spotlights on hiring and pay

Blogs:

  • Making Skills a Reality in Your Organization
  • Norsk Hydro Empowers Talent with AI Capabilities on Journey to Become a Skills-Based Organization

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Integrate SAP BTP Cloud Foundry with IBM Instana
entry.content:
How about adding additional monitoring and performance visibility to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) Cloud Foundry applications?
Whether you're working with SAP Ui5/Fiori, Node.js, or Java Spring Boot applications, this guide provides you with interactive steps to easily install the IBM® Instana agent and start monitoring your apps in real time.

Steps:

You need to get a SaaS license offering of IBM Instana for SAP Business Technology Platform. 

Plug & Play for SAP Ui5/Fiori Applications

  1. Insert the Instana Script:
    • Open the index.html of your SAP Ui5 application. It will be inside the webapp directory.


      • Add the following script to enable Instana monitoring: 
        <script>
  (function(s,t,a,n){s[t]||(s[t]=a,n=s[a]=function(){n.q.push(arguments)},
  n.q=[],n.v=2,n.l=1*new Date)})(window,"InstanaEumObject","ineum");
  ineum('reportingUrl', ''); // Add your reporting URL here
  ineum('key', ''); // Add your Instana key here
  ineum('trackSessions');
</script>
<script defer crossorigin="anonymous" src="https://eum.instana.io/1.6.6/eum.min.js" integrity=""></script>
  2. You're All Set:

       Deploy your application, and the Instana agent will begin monitoring immediately.

Plug & Play for Node.js Applications

  1. Install the Instana Package:

    • If using npm, run:
    npm install --save @instana/serverless-collector
    • If using yarn, run:
    yarn add @instana/serverless-collector

  2. Configure the Manifest File:

    • Update your manifest.yaml with the necessary Instana configurations. The manifest location will be at the root directory of the application:

  • applications:
- name: node-demo-api
  host: node-demo-api
  domain: cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com
  buildpacks:
    - nodejs_buildpack # optional
  env:
    NODE_OPTIONS: '--require ./node_modules/@instana/serverless-collector'
    INSTANA_AGENT_KEY: '' # Add your agent key
    INSTANA_ENDPOINT_URL: '' # Add the endpoint URL
    INSTANA_LOG_LEVEL: '' # Set the log level

     

Plug & Play for Java Spring Boot Applications

  1. Download the Instana Collector:

    • Obtain the latest standalone JVM collector from the Instana repository: Instana Collector Download

  2. Update the Manifest File:

    • Add the following configurations to your manifest.yml. The manifest location will be at the root directory of the application:

 

applications:
- name: rest-demo-app
  path: ./target/rest-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
  host: rest-demo-app
  domain: cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com
  env:
    JBP_CONFIG_JAVA_OPTS: 'java_opts: "-javaagent:BOOT-INF/lib/standalone-collector-jvm-[version].jar"'
    INSTANA_AGENT_KEY: '' # Add your agent key
    INSTANA_ENDPOINT_URL: '' # Add the endpoint URL
    INSTANA_LOG_LEVEL: 'TRACE' # Set the log level

 

Final Steps

Deploy Your Applications: - Push your applications to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry with the Instana agent configurations enabled. For SAP UI5/Fiori apps,i.e., multi target applications:

   mbt build

and then Deploy mta archives.

For other cloud native applications, do a

cf push

Monitor in Real-Time: - Use the Instana dashboard to monitor your applications in real-time, providing you with insights and actionable data to optimize performance and troubleshoot issues. For reference, we are leveraging the REST calls failures here:

By following these interactive steps, you can effortlessly integrate Instana with your SAP Cloud Foundry applications, providing additional monitoring and performance visibility .

Happy coding and monitoring!

entry.title: Experience Report: ABAP Code Retreat 2024 – A Beginner's Perspective
entry.content:
ABAP Code Retreat 2024 at Inwerken AG

On September 10th, 2024, the ABAP Code Retreat took place once again in Isernhagen (Hannover). Originally launched in 2016 by @majcon and @ceedee666 this event gathers ABAP developers to code and learn together. The focus of the retreat was on Test Driven Development (TDD), with challenges like "Game of Life" or the "Bowling Kata" used to demonstrate this methodology. The event at Inwerken AG was organized and coordinated by @EnnoWulff , who ensured everything ran smoothly. As beginners, we would like to share our impressions and experiences from this inspiring workshop.

What were our expectations?

Before the event, we had mixed feelings about the ABAP Code Retreat. On one hand, we were excited to expand our knowledge, on the other, we were concerned about being overwhelmed as newcomers. We were curious to see how developers from other companies tackle problems and expected to be challenged, both by the topic and by engaging with more experienced developers.

How did it turn out?

Our initial concerns quickly disappeared once we received a thorough introduction to Test Driven Development. This helped us understand the goal of the workshop and the importance of the methodology. Thanks to the support from both the other participants and the moderator, we never felt left behind and were able to follow along at a comfortable pace.

Pair Programming was one of the biggest challenges, but also one of the most rewarding experiences. Working as a team was essential, and it helped us organize our thought process around solving problems. The alternating roles were particularly beneficial. While one person coded, the other could think through the next steps. This shift in perspective allowed both partners to contribute in different, valuable ways.

It was also fascinating to see how developers from other companies approached and solved similar challenges. And, of course, it was a great opportunity to make new connections that might extend beyond the workshop itself.

What did we learn?

A key takeaway from the retreat was an introduction to Test Driven Development (TDD). This approach is very different from typical development workflows. Instead of jumping straight into coding, TDD starts with the question: "What should my method produce as the final result?". The first step is writing a unit test that checks the desired output. Only after that do you implement the method. Finally, the unit test is used to verify whether the method's logic is correct or needs refining.

This reverse way of thinking made it much easier to code in a focused manner and spot potential problems early on. It was an "aha" moment to see how continuous testing during development.

Who should attend this workshop?

We recommend this workshop to developers looking to explore new approaches and improve their workflow. It is especially useful for ABAP developers with little to no experience in Test Driven Development (TDD), offering a hands-on opportunity to practice new methodologies that can be easily applied to everyday work.

Additionally, developers seeking to broaden their network and exchange ideas with colleagues from other companies will find this event beneficial. The collaboration during Pair Programming and the sharing of ideas provides a chance to view problems from multiple perspectives.

The ABAP Code Retreat was a valuable experience for us, helping us grow both professionally and personally. We’re looking forward to future events where we can further deepen our knowledge and make new connections.

~Ben

~@l_e_n_a 

~@Hoolya
